description: Mandatory DQ rule patterns for consumable zone specs. @dq-rule-writer must evaluate each pattern and either write a rule or document why it was skipped.

patterns.description: One value per entity-metric-period at the declared grain. If a consumable table has a defined grain (e.g., one annual revenue per company-year), there must be exactly one row per grain combination.
patterns.sql_template: SELECT {grain_columns}, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM {table} GROUP BY {grain_columns} HAVING cnt > 1
patterns.when: Always — every consumable table has a declared grain

patterns.description: Values that violate domain constraints. Revenue cannot be negative, percentages cannot exceed 100%, counts cannot be fractional. Domain-context.md defines what constraints apply.
patterns.when: When domain-context.md or the logical model defines value constraints for any metric

patterns.description: Related tables agree on shared dimensions. If two consumable tables share an entity or time dimension, their values for shared attributes must agree.
patterns.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {table_a} a JOIN {table_b} b ON {join_condition} WHERE {disagreement_condition}
patterns.when: When the spec produces multiple related tables or references existing consumable tables

patterns.description: Known-correct values match pipeline output. At least 3 independently verifiable values from authoritative sources must match the pipeline output within tolerance.
patterns.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {table} WHERE {entity_condition} AND {metric_condition} AND {period_condition} AND ABS({value_col} - {expected_value}) > {tolerance}
patterns.when: Always — every consumable spec must have a golden dataset before @staff-engineer review

patterns.description: After collision resolution, no duplicate concepts per entity-period. If concept normalization maps multiple source codes to the same canonical concept, collision resolution must produce exactly one value per grain.
patterns.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT {entity_id}, canonical_concept, {period_fields}, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM {table} GROUP BY {entity_id}, canonical_concept, {period_fields} HAVING cnt > 1)
patterns.when: When the spec uses concept normalization with multi-source mappings

patterns.description: Mapped concepts cover >= threshold of base rows. If concept normalization is in use, a minimum percentage of source records should map to a canonical concept.
patterns.sql_template: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE canonical_concept IS NOT NULL) AS DOUBLE) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) FROM {table}
patterns.when: When concept normalization is in use
